tagline: Turn your car into a lifesaver

notifications.monitoringBody: Accident monitoring is active in the background

danger.title: Delete Log
danger.subtitle: Permanently delete all reports (cannot be undone)

pdf.subtitle: Smart accident analysis system
pdf.title: Detailed Accident Report
pdf.liabilityHint: (0 means the user is not at fault, 100 means fully at fault)
pdf.croquisNote: Sketch auto-generated from sensor data — dimensions and angles are approximate
pdf.matchTitle: This accident was linked to another user's report
pdf.matchDesc: Another Strix user involved in the same accident was found.
pdf.adjustLabel: Liability adjustment based on sensor data
pdf.footer1: This report was generated automatically by the Strix smart accident analysis system.
pdf.footer2: The report is based on precise vehicle-motion readings and does not replace the official traffic report from the competent authorities.

sysNotes.crossAdjusted: (Liability adjusted after cross-checking data with the other party's phone and applying traffic rules)
sysNotes.directionUncalibrated: Direction is approximate — phone not mounted/calibrated; confidence was lowered for credibility.

otherParty.desc: Other-party analysis: a {{vehicle}} likely approached {{dir}} at an estimated speed of {{speed}} km/h. Impact force was {{force}}.
otherParty.wasAccelerating: Analysis indicates the other party was accelerating and did not attempt to brake.
otherParty.wasBraking: Analysis indicates the other party attempted to brake before impact.

landing.description: The smart accident detection and real-time analysis system to ensure your safety on the road.

home.crashDetected: Crash detected — tap to view report
home.noAccidentsSub: Start a monitoring session to detect and log incidents
home.deleteAllConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete all accident reports? This action cannot be undone.

session.title: Live Monitoring
session.calibrating: Calibrating… detection starts in a moment
session.webWarning: Sensors only work on a real iPhone. Scan the QR code with the Expo Go app.

report.title: Accident Report
report.qualityDirectionApprox: Direction is approximate — phone not mounted/calibrated; confidence lowered.
report.qualityAccelSaturated: Impact force may be a lower bound (phone sensor saturation).
report.deleteReportMessage: This record will be permanently deleted.
report.deleteConfirm: This record will be permanently deleted.
report.wasAssessmentFair: Was the liability estimate fair and accurate?
report.legalNote: This analysis is for informational purposes only and does not constitute a legal judgment. G-force measurements are estimated based on phone sensors and may not be 100% accurate.

liability.rearImpactTitle: Rear Impact — Full Responsibility on Other Party
liability.frontImpactTitle: Frontal Impact — Initial Responsibility on Driver
liability.cornerFrontTitle: Front {{side}} Corner Impact — Intersection or Merge Collision
liability.sideSuddenIntrusionFactor1: The sharp and sudden change in motion proves the impact came abruptly from the {{side}} side.
liability.sideSuddenIntrusionFactor2: The vehicle that suddenly intruded into the {{lane}} bears primary responsibility.
liability.sideLaneChangeSelfTitle: {{side}} Side Impact — Scrape or Dangerous Proximity
liability.sideLaneChangeSelfFactor1: Gradual convergence and weak sudden impact force suggest a scrape while moving.
liability.sideLaneChangeSelfFactor2: This pattern implies a superficial scrape or dangerous proximity due to insufficient lateral clearance.
liability.sideAmbiguousFactor1: {{side}} side impact while moving — potential shared responsibility.
liability.sideAmbiguousFactor2: Unable to pinpoint the swerving or scraping vehicle accurately via sensors alone; referring to dashcams is recommended.
liability.sideAmbiguousFactor3: High impact force indicates excessive speed by one of the parties during the side scrape.
liability.cornerRearTitle: Rear {{side}} Corner Impact — Other Party Bears Major Fault
liability.cornerRearFactor1: Rear {{side}} corner impact — suggests the other party was attempting to overtake or evade.
liability.cornerRearFactor2: The vehicle was stationary — responsibility lies with the striking party.
liability.cornerRearFactor3: A lane change by the driver was detected — this may have contributed by swerving in front of the other party.
liability.cornerRearFactor4: Sudden braking was detected — the rear party failed to maintain a safe distance.
liability.unknownFactor1: Impact direction could not be accurately determined by sensors — the phone might not be securely mounted.
liability.unknownFactor2: It is recommended to recalibrate the phone's position horizontally within a dedicated car mount.
liability.impactCount: {{count}} consecutive impacts detected — indicates a multi-stage crash or rollover.
liability.impactZoneFrontCorner: Impact at the front {{side}} corner suggests a lane merge or intersection collision.
liability.impactZoneRearCorner: Impact at the rear {{side}} corner suggests the striking party was trying to evade.
liability.rolloverFactor: Violent vehicle rotation detected, indicating a rollover.
liability.roundaboutPriorityTitle: Side Impact in Roundabout — Priority to the Circulating Vehicle
liability.faultVerdict0: The other party bears full responsibility
liability.faultVerdict25: The other party bears the majority of the responsibility
liability.faultVerdict50: Responsibility is shared equally between both parties
liability.faultVerdict75: The driver bears the majority of the responsibility
liability.jerkNote: A sudden acceleration of {{jerk}} g/s indicates an abrupt external force.
liability.brakingNote: An attempt to brake ({{duration}}s) was detected before the impact.
liability.descriptionFormat: {{dir}} collision at {{g}}g while traveling at {{speed}} km/h. Severity: {{severity}}.{{jerkNote}}{{brakingNote}} {{faultVerdict}} ({{fault}}% on driver).

feedback.rateSub: Was the liability estimate fair and accurate?
feedback.accurateThanks: Accurate — Thank you for your feedback
feedback.inaccurateNote: Inaccurate — Will be used to improve the engine
feedback.legalNote: This estimate is informational and not a legal judgment — consult official authorities.

settings.title: Settings
settings.gyroscopeSub: Reduce false alerts by confirming rotation
settings.sampleRateSub: Higher rate means more accuracy and battery usage
settings.sensitivitySub: Acceleration threshold required to trigger detection
settings.hintLow: Road vibrations may be logged as incidents — use in sports cars
settings.hintHigh: Detects only strong impacts — suitable for rough terrain
settings.hintNormal: Suitable for everyday use in regular vehicles
settings.aboutDescription: Strix uses the Accelerometer and Gyroscope together in a Sensor Fusion system with a High-Pass Filter algorithm to isolate collision motion from gravity. The v4 analysis engine calculates peak G-force, jerk, impact direction, speed, braking, and rotation data together to estimate liability with higher accuracy.
settings.detectionSub: Choose sensing precision and impact-force threshold
settings.disclaimer: Data is stored locally on device only. This app is for informational purposes and is not a substitute for official authorities.

directionKnown: The impact direction is clear and accurately determined.
The collision angle was precisely tracked by sensors.
The trajectory of the impact force is confirmed.
The exact point of impact has been identified with high precision.
directionUnknown: The impact direction is unclear — the phone might not be securely mounted.
Scattered directional readings, the phone may have been dropped during the crash.
Impact angle cannot be accurately determined (possible free movement of the phone).
highGForce: Extremely high impact force ({{g}}g) confirms a severe accident.
A violent shock of {{g}}g was recorded, indicating a heavy collision.
The collision produced {{g}}g of force, signaling a high-severity crash.
mediumGForce: Moderate impact force ({{g}}g) indicates a collision occurred.
A {{g}}g shock was detected, confirming a strong contact or scrape.
G-force indicators ({{g}}g) point to a moderate severity incident.
lowGForce: Low impact force ({{g}}g) — could be a speed bump or phone drop.
A minor vibration ({{g}}g) was recorded, which may not necessarily be a traffic accident.
Very light shock, likely due to minor contact or sudden movement.
speedHigh: Vehicle motion confirmed (speed at impact: {{speed}} km/h).
The vehicle was traveling at {{speed}} km/h at the moment of the crash.
The impact occurred while moving at an estimated {{speed}} km/h.
speedLow: The vehicle was moving very slowly or almost stationary ({{speed}} km/h).
Speed was negligible ({{speed}} km/h); vehicle was parked or creeping.
A very low speed ({{speed}} km/h) was recorded at impact.
jerkHigh: Sudden and sharp change in motion ({{jerk}} g/s) confirms an abrupt crash.
Extreme instantaneous acceleration ({{jerk}} g/s), solid proof of sudden stop or external impact.
High jerk force ({{jerk}} g/s) points to a collision without prior warning.
spinDetected: Vehicle spin or deflection detected immediately upon impact — conclusive evidence of a crash.
The rotation sensor confirms the vehicle body twisted due to the impact force.
The vehicle lost its straight trajectory and veered upon collision.
shakeDetected: Sharp structural vibration recorded simultaneously with the impact.
Noticeable fluctuation in gyroscope readings indicates vehicle shaking.
Strong structural vibrations were documented at the moment of collision.
brakingDetected: Braking attempt detected before the crash — indicates awareness and evasion effort.
Data shows brake application right before the impact.
The driver attempted an abrupt stop mere seconds before the collision.
rearBase: Standard traffic rule: A rear-end collision places full responsibility on the striking vehicle for failing to maintain a safe distance.
According to traffic laws, the rear vehicle bears 100% fault for failing to stop in time.
The impact occurred from the rear, placing full legal liability on the trailing vehicle.
rearBraking: Despite an attempted stop by the driver, maintaining a safe distance remains the rear vehicle's responsibility.
The front vehicle's sudden stop does not exempt the rear vehicle from the fault of insufficient following distance.
The driver braked suddenly, but regulations require the rear party to leave enough space for a full stop.
rearSpeedLow: The vehicle was stationary or nearly stopped at impact, entirely confirming the rear party's fault.
The crash happened while parked or creeping, leaving 100% fault on the rear approaching vehicle.
Rear-ended while nearly stationary; there is no excuse for the rear vehicle other than distraction.
rearSpeedHigh: The crash occurred while driving at {{speed}} km/h, and the rear party failed to match the deceleration.
The vehicle was traveling at {{speed}} km/h, and the rear party did not maintain a distance suitable for this speed.
Driving speed ({{speed}} km/h) requires a large safety margin, which was neglected by the rear party.
rearGForceHigh: The severe impact force confirms excessive speed or total distraction by the rear party.
The violent shock indicates no attempt by the rear party to decelerate at all.
The intensity of the rear strike suggests the other driver was not paying attention or was speeding excessively.
frontBase: Direct frontal collision — typically indicates a failure to maintain a safe following distance.
Frontal impact holds the driver initially responsible for failing to stop the vehicle.
A front-end crash suggests the driver hit the object ahead due to poor braking distance estimation.
frontNote: Note: The fault percentage may change if advanced analysis proves extenuating circumstances (e.g., the other party's sudden stop or swerve).
The final fault ratio will be confirmed upon cross-referencing with the other party's data, if available.
Fault percentage is adjustable based on environmental conditions and the involved vehicle's kinematics.
frontBraking: A braking attempt was recorded before impact — but it does not absolve the responsibility to maintain safe distance.
Brake application was too late and insufficient to avoid the frontal crash.
The driver tried to stop before the crash, reducing the likelihood of intent but maintaining the distance fault.
frontSpeedLow: The collision occurred at low speed (during parking, in traffic, or at an intersection).
A light frontal crash indicates a misjudgment during slow maneuvers.
The impact happened while moving slowly, suggesting a minor slip or congested traffic.
frontSpeedHigh: Driving at {{speed}} km/h requires an adequate stopping distance which was not provided.
Due to the speed ({{speed}} km/h), the driver lacked sufficient time to evade the frontal impact.
The recorded speed of {{speed}} km/h obligates the driver to leave a larger gap ahead.
frontGForceHigh: The severe impact force confirms a massive speed difference between the two vehicles at collision.
High impact intensity reflects a lack of significant deceleration by the driver prior to the crash.
A strong frontal shock points to a total loss of control or distraction from the road ahead.
cornerBase: Collision at the {{side}} front corner — likely an intersection crash or lane merge issue.
A {{side}} corner strike usually indicates an improper turn or unsafe merge.
An impact on the {{side}} frontal area suggests a dispute over right-of-way or a lane change.
cornerYawHigh: A lane change by the driver was detected before the crash with a rotation rate of {{yawRate}}°/s — suggesting they swerved into the other vehicle.
The gyroscope recorded a clear steering turn ({{yawRate}}°/s), increasing the probability of the driver's fault due to swerving.
The driver changed lanes in the final moments before the crash, raising their liability for the collision.
cornerYawLow: The vehicle was traveling straight — it is likely the other party entered the lane or cut across the road.
Data shows no swerve from the driver, implying the other vehicle obstructed their path.
Since the driver maintained their lane, the likely intrusion came from the other party.
cornerJerkHigh: The crash was sudden and sharp — suggesting the other party abruptly entered the intersection.
The sudden spike in jerk indicates the other vehicle unexpectedly blocked the road.
There was no room to anticipate the crash given the suddenness and force of the impact.
cornerSpeedLow: The low speed suggests a congested intersection or a parking maneuver error.
A slow impact points to spatial misjudgment by one or both parties.
At such low speeds, the crash is often the result of traffic jams and vehicles tailgating.
sideRoll: The collision caused a lateral tilt of the vehicle body — confirming the strength of the side impact.
Vehicle imbalance (roll) detection indicates a strong crash that pushed it sideways.
The side impact was forceful enough to cause a roll sway detected by the gyroscope.
sideLaneChangeConfirmed: Actual steering wheel swerve detected ({{yawRate}}°/s) — confirming the driver changed lanes.
The gyroscope proves the vehicle moved to another lane ({{yawRate}}°/s), placing high liability on the driver.
The driver turned right or left at the moment of impact, confirming they caused the lane intrusion.
sideLaneChangeFault: The driver bears primary responsibility for changing lanes without ensuring it was clear.
The law holds the lane-changer at fault for swerving into vehicles already established in the lane.
Changing lanes into the other vehicle is considered the direct cause of the side collision.
sideLowSpeed: A {{side}} side collision at low speed — possibly an intersection, roundabout, or parking lot incident.
A slow {{side}} contact suggests a scrape in heavy traffic or during a turn.
A {{side}} side strike at low speeds requires advanced analysis to determine right-of-way.
sideLowSpeedNote: Right-of-way determination depends on road type and traffic context — this will be analyzed via advanced principles.
To ascertain exact fault percentages, the system measures the Micro-Kinematic signature.
Right-of-way is definitively determined based on cross-referencing data between the involved parties.
sideJerkHigh: The sudden impact force indicates the other party entered aggressively without slowing down.
A sharp lateral jerk means the other party struck the vehicle abruptly and forcefully.
A side collision with no braking by the other party points to recklessness or total inattention.
sideSuddenIntrusion: The sharp and sudden change in motion proves the impact came abruptly from the {{side}} side.
A sudden lateral movement was detected, indicating an abrupt collision from the {{side}} direction.
The high lateral acceleration confirms that the impact occurred suddenly and directly on the {{side}} side.
sideSuddenIntrusionFault: The vehicle that suddenly intruded into the {{lane}} bears primary responsibility.
Sudden swerving into the {{lane}} is considered the main cause of this collision.
Abruptly entering the {{lane}} places the primary fault on the swerving vehicle.
sideLaneChangeSelf1: Gradual convergence and weak sudden impact force suggest a scrape while moving.
A moderate vibration profile suggests a gradual side-to-side scrape rather than a direct hit.
Sensor logs point to a superficial scrape along the vehicle side rather than a high-energy impact.
sideLaneChangeSelf2: This pattern implies a superficial scrape or dangerous proximity due to insufficient lateral clearance.
The absence of sharp shock vectors suggests a side scrape due to narrow lateral spacing.
The incident is likely caused by failure to maintain safe lateral distance, resulting in a scrape.
sideAmbiguous: {{side}} side impact while moving — potential shared responsibility.
A mutual swipe was registered on the {{side}} side, suggesting shared fault while both vehicles were in motion.
Side contact occurred while both vehicles were moving, indicating a high likelihood of mutual fault.
sideAmbiguousNote: Unable to pinpoint the swerving or scraping vehicle accurately via sensors alone; referring to dashcams is recommended.
Lacking clear swerve orientation, reviewing dashcam footage is advised to establish initial swerve.
Identifying the primary swerving vehicle requires additional visual proof or on-site testimonies.
sideAmbiguousGHigh: High impact force indicates excessive speed by one of the parties during the side scrape.
High lateral energy points to excessive speed relative to the lateral distance between vehicles.
A high force recording indicates that one of the parties was driving too fast during the swipe.
cornerRearBase: Rear {{side}} corner impact — suggests the other party was attempting to overtake or evade.
An impact on the rear {{side}} corner suggests an unsafe overtaking maneuver by the striking vehicle.
Impact at the rear {{side}} corner points to the other vehicle failing to clear your car while swerving.
cornerRearStationary: The vehicle was stationary — responsibility lies with the striking party.
Since your vehicle was completely stopped, 100% of the fault lies with the striking vehicle.
A stationary state indicates the other party is fully liable for failing to avoid your stopped vehicle.
cornerRearLaneChange: A lane change by the driver was detected — this may have contributed by swerving in front of the other party.
Gyroscope logs register a turn by the driver, suggesting they might have swerved into the other vehicle's path.
A swerve was detected on your vehicle, indicating potential contribution to the collision via lane change.
cornerRearBraking: Sudden braking was detected — the rear party failed to maintain a safe distance.
Although sudden braking was detected, the rear vehicle is responsible for maintaining a safe stopping distance.
Braking was registered just before impact, confirming the driver tried to avoid a tailgating vehicle.

advancedAnalysis.angularInstability: Angular instability: Sharp swerve of {{yawRate}}°/s at the moment of impact.
advancedAnalysis.straightPath: The vehicle was moving in a steady straight line.
advancedAnalysis.principle2: Principle 2: Force Vectors (Direction and motion confirmation)
advancedAnalysis.brakingVector: Strong braking vector: Sharp deceleration of {{accelX}}g.
advancedAnalysis.rearPush: Push effect: Rear force accounting for {{ratio}} of the total force.
advancedAnalysis.stopBeforeCrash: The vehicle came to a complete stop right before the crash.
advancedAnalysis.roundabout: Kinematic pattern indicates driving in a roundabout.
advancedAnalysis.intersection: Kinematic pattern indicates crossing an intersection.
advancedAnalysis.highway: High speed and straight path indicate highway driving.
advancedAnalysis.priorityRoundabout: Roundabout Priority: The vehicle was inside the roundabout (fault lies with the entering vehicle).
advancedAnalysis.scrapeDetected: Side Scrape: Prolonged friction lasting {{duration}}ms instead of an instantaneous shock.
advancedAnalysis.yawJerkSync: Synchronization between sudden jerk and angular deflection: Confirms the impact force and source.
advancedAnalysis.varianceHigh: High variance: Indicates collision with an uneven object or repeated scraping.
advancedAnalysis.hardBrakingPreCrash: Hard braking before crash: Indicates delayed awareness.
advancedAnalysis.suddenAccelPreCrash: Sudden acceleration before crash: May indicate an improper overtake or evasion attempt.
advancedAnalysis.steadyPreCrash: Driving was stable and steady in the seconds preceding the accident.
advancedAnalysis.evasionAttempt: Evasion attempt: Braking coupled with angular deflection.
advancedAnalysis.postImpactDeflection: Path deflection post-impact by an angle of {{angle}}°.
advancedAnalysis.multiImpact: {{count}} secondary impacts detected (rebound or multiple collisions).
advancedAnalysis.directionConfirmed: The vehicle's deflection direction matches the estimated impact direction.

otherParty.analysis: Other Party Analysis — Inferred from sensor data
otherParty.approachingFrom: Approached from an angle of {{angle}}° at an estimated speed of {{speed}} km/h. Impact force: {{impact}}g. Was {{state1}} and {{state2}}.

falseAlarm.modalSubtitle: Select the reason why the app recorded this accident by mistake
falseAlarm.modalSub: Select the reason why the app recorded this accident by mistake
falseAlarm.selectReasonPrompt: Please select a reason for the report
falseAlarm.thankYouMessage: Thank you for your report, this will help us improve system accuracy.
falseAlarm.errorMessage: An error occurred while submitting the report, please try again.
falseAlarm.alertMissingReason: Please select a reason for the report
falseAlarm.alertSuccess: Thank you for your report, this will help us improve system accuracy.
falseAlarm.alertError: An error occurred while submitting the report, please try again.

assessment.title: Official Report Comparison
assessment.najmSub: Select your fault percentage according to the official report
assessment.differenceMatch: App estimate matches the official estimate ✓
assessment.differenceValue: Difference between App and official estimate: {{diff}}%
assessment.notesPlaceholder: Write additional details about the accident or reason for the difference...
assessment.disclaimer: The reporting-authority options shown above are for internal assessment and for comparing the app's estimates with official reports, in order to improve the algorithm's accuracy. Strix does not represent any official authority or insurance company, and does not claim any affiliation, partnership, or official endorsement with them. Entered data is the user's responsibility.
assessment.alertMissing: Please select the fault percentage from the official report
assessment.alertError: An error occurred while saving the assessment

advancedAnalysisFactors.evasive: Evasive maneuver detected: Sudden swerve ({{yawRate}}°/s) accompanied by braking before impact — evidence of evasion attempt.
advancedAnalysisFactors.suddenYaw: Sudden lane change detected ({{yawRate}}°/s) before impact without braking — suggests the driver swerved.
advancedAnalysisFactors.straight: The vehicle was traveling in a straight line before impact — no lane change detected.
advancedAnalysisFactors.rearPush: Force vector analysis shows a longitudinal component of {{ratio}}% — the other party struck at an angle, not perpendicularly.
advancedAnalysisFactors.roundabout: Curved motion analysis indicates the accident occurred in a roundabout — priority belongs to the circulating vehicle.
advancedAnalysisFactors.stationary: The vehicle was completely stationary at impact — full responsibility on the moving party.
advancedAnalysisFactors.scrapeSync: Side scrape/friction detected (duration: {{duration}}ms) (Confirmed by Jerk-Gyro sync) — superficial collision from the other party.
advancedAnalysisFactors.scrapeNoSync: Side scrape/friction detected (duration: {{duration}}ms) — superficial collision from the other party.
advancedAnalysisFactors.preCrashEvasive: Full evasive maneuver (hard braking + swerve) detected before impact — conclusive evidence of good intent to avoid the crash.
advancedAnalysisFactors.preCrashBraking: Hard braking detected before impact — evidence of driver's awareness and attempt to stop.
advancedAnalysisFactors.preCrashAccel: Sudden acceleration detected before impact — may indicate inattention or attempting to beat a traffic light.
advancedAnalysisFactors.preCrashSteady: The vehicle was driving completely steady before impact — no abnormal behavior.
advancedAnalysisFactors.postStopped: The vehicle remained in place post-impact and did not move (evidence of complete stop).
advancedAnalysisFactors.postDrift: Vehicle drifted {{dir}} post-impact with a force of {{g}}g.
advancedAnalysisFactors.postRotation: Vehicle rotated post-impact by {{rate}}°/s (loss of control due to collision).
advancedAnalysisFactors.postSecondary: {{count}} secondary impacts detected after the crash (rebound or hitting other obstacles).

liabilityMeter.title: Liability Estimate
liabilityMeter.verdictOtherParty: The other party bears primary responsibility
liabilityMeter.disclaimer: For informational purposes only — not a legal judgment
